ops.title: Fix-waves W1–W5 review rebuttals — rejected findings and why
ops.body:
Adversarial-review findings REJECTED during the 2026-07-14 quality-restoration waves, with rebuttals — cite these before re-raising any of them:

1. **"Deleted-key grep hits remain for layover_style/program_preference"** (W1 refuter) — the remaining hits are the rejection tests themselves, asserting the keys are now refused. Exactly what should exist.
2. **"Constraint-free output is not bit-identical: mixed_cabin_minutes semantics changed"** (W1 round-3 refuter) — the below-preferred-only `_cabin_fit` semantics and the `below_cabin_minutes` rename are the deliberate, decided fix (the old any-different-cabin count scored first-class segments as misses against a business preference, which would have poisoned the primary-clears guard). Not a regression.
3. **"Stale bridge.json fails as a bare KeyError; add a read-site catch or UsageError"** (W3 review, minor) — fail-loud on stale artifacts is house style (v2 cutover precedent); W4's walker `stderr_tail` surfaces the traceback self-diagnosingly; a read-site catch is defensive scar tissue the styleguide forbids.
4. **"Make the band branch airtight with `a_total < b_total` for negative totals"** (W5 refuter, minor) — negative costs are corrupt data (nonnegative through the whole pipeline); the accepted fix was stating the nonnegativity precondition in the assert comment, not a guard against an impossible state.
5. **Zero-axis intransitivity and negative-total antisymmetry** (W2 refuter, informational) — both require vectors the pipeline cannot produce (positive-sum mileage components; cash only when nonzero). No guards on impossible states; recorded here instead.

Accepted-and-applied findings live in the wave comments on the fix-waves task (637198b) and the ranking-lanes doctrine (2954567a).
